context: Tests whether the answer uses the iOS 26 Swift Charts 3D APIs for a bivariate surface without overusing 3D for ordinary charts.

checklist.name: Chart3D API Choice
checklist.description: Uses Chart3D with SurfacePlot for the iOS 26 bivariate surface instead of a 2D Chart with LinePlot or AreaPlot.

checklist.name: Surface Function Shape
checklist.description: Shows or describes SurfacePlot with x, y, and z labels and a closure that returns y from x and z.

checklist.name: Stable 3D Domains
checklist.description: Sets or recommends explicit chartXScale, chartYScale, and chartZScale domains.

checklist.name: Interactive Pose
checklist.description: Mentions Chart3DPose or chart3DPose for interactive inspection.

checklist.name: 3D Appropriateness
checklist.description: Notes that Chart3D is for real spatial or surface data, not decoration for ordinary 2D comparisons.
